definition: Commodity markets are sending bifurcated signals that refute monolithic inflation frameworks. LME copper is in persistent backwardation ($30-80/t), crude oil in $1-2/bbl contango, aluminum in modest contango. There is no unified commodity super-cycle.
evidence: Directly observable in futures market term structures
generalist_01 positive commodity-equity correlation (+0.2 to +0.3) confirms demand-pull not supply-push
Oil-energy inflation pathway low probability given OPEC+ spare capacity 5-6 Mb/d
Remaining risk channel is narrow: metals/power-driven inflation from copper and data center demand
open_questions: Could metals/power-driven inflation alone trigger a rate shock?
How does bifurcation affect traditional commodity-equity correlation models?
